Maternal Child Adolescent Health Program
Maternal Child Adolescent Health Program (MCAH)
Goals of the
MCAH
Program:
The Maternal, Child and Adolescent Health (
MCAH
) program strives to develop systems that protect and improve the health of women, infants, children, adolescents and their families.
The goals and objectives of Colusa County
MCAH
program incorporate local problems identify by the 5 year needs assessment. Some areas of focus are:
Improving access to prenatal care in the first trimester.
Oral health
Child passenger safety
Improving breastfeeding initiation and duration rates.
Developmental screening for children 0-5 years.
Sudden Unexplained Infant Death (
SUID
) and Sudden Infant Death Syndrome (
SIDS
).
Obesity among the
MCAH
population
Adolescent health
The Colusa County
MCAH
program collaborates with many community partners in a variety activities to improve the health of women, children and their families.
Services:
Assistance with navigating the health care system
Referral to local resources
Breastfeeding education and support by a Certified Lactation Education Counselor
Provide health education material on maternal, child and adolescent health topics
Child passenger safety education and car seat checkups
Grief and bereavement support services for families experiencing a sudden and unexpected infant death.
To access
MCAH
services visit the Colusa County Public Health office or call (530) 458-0380 or 1-800-655-3110
Colusa County Public Health Division
251 E. Webster Street
Colusa CA 95932
The Colusa County
MCAH
program can help you find services and resources for you and your family.
Are you pregnant?
Do you need help finding a prenatal care provider? Do want to know more about maternal health and local resources for you and your baby?
Visit the
MCAH
program at the Colusa County Public Health office or call (530) 458-0380 or 1 (800) 655-3110
Click on the links listed below to view the pregnancy resources available for Colusa County women.
